appraisal_events.evidence: « La présence de Gilberte dans un salon… était un obstacle… on prit l'habitude de ne plus le nommer… celle qui aurait dû… se trouva hâter et consommer l'œuvre de la mort et de l'oubli. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator asserts that, contrary to Swann’s paternal hope, Gilberte’s social presence hastens the effacement of his memory, reducing his posthumous presence in conversation.

status_effects.explanation: Swann is locally excluded from salon talk and memory; his name ceases to be mentioned.
